context: Tests whether the agent uses 1Password Connect for runtime application secrets (not GitHub repository secrets), OIDC for cloud authentication (not long-lived credentials), enforces correct secret hygiene in workflow steps, and sets minimal workflow permissions.

checklist.name: 1Password for runtime env
checklist.description: Uses 1password/load-secrets-action (or the load-1password-env composite action) to render runtime application secrets, rather than referencing them directly from `secrets.*` in the deploy step

checklist.name: op:// references in template
checklist.description: An env template file is committed to the repo containing `op://` references (not plaintext secrets), e.g. `op://vault/item/field` format

checklist.name: OIDC for cloud credentials
checklist.description: Uses aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials (with `role-to-assume`) or equivalent OIDC mechanism for cloud access, NOT a long-lived AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID / AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY in secrets

checklist.name: id-token write at job level
checklist.description: The deploy job grants `id-token: write` permission at the job level (not at the workflow root), and the workflow root uses `contents: read` only

checklist.name: Root permissions minimal
checklist.description: The workflow-level `permissions:` block grants only `contents: read` (or is absent and the job-level grants are used exclusively)

checklist.name: No secrets as CLI flags
checklist.description: Secret values are passed to commands via environment variables (the `env:` block), NOT as positional or named command-line arguments

checklist.name: No env file dump
checklist.description: No workflow step uses `cat`, `echo`, or `print` to output the full contents of a rendered `.env` or secrets file

checklist.name: account-id in vars not secrets
checklist.description: Non-sensitive identifiers like Cloudflare account ID or AWS account ID are referenced from `vars.*` (not `secrets.*`) in the workflow

checklist.name: Fine-grained PAT
checklist.description: Any PAT used for cross-repo operations is stored with a scoped name (e.g. `TAP_GITHUB_TOKEN`, `OPS_TRIGGER_TOKEN`) and the explanation does NOT reference a classic `ghp_` PAT or an unscoped token

checklist.name: GitHub secrets only for bootstrap
checklist.description: The written explanation or comments describe GitHub secrets as holding only CI-access credentials (e.g. OP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_TOKEN, cloud OIDC role ARN) — not application runtime env values

checklist.name: Env rendered in runner not VPS
checklist.description: For VPS/container deployments, the env file is rendered on the GitHub Actions runner (not on the VPS), and the rendered file is transferred to the VPS via scp or equivalent
